text: From 2006, when the first examples of primary amine-thiourea organocatalysts were first shown to catalyze asymmetric reactions with high enantioselectivity, the field of primary amine-thiourea organocatalysis has grown to become a significant area of research for synthesizing hundreds of chiral organic molecules of significant structural complexity and diversity. Building on the synergistic pairing of primary amine activation modes with the thiourea hydrogen-bond donating and accepting capabilities, multifaceted catalyst structures have been synthesized that facilitate the efficient catalysis of an ever increasing number of chiroselective reactions. This review article covers the achievements in this field focusing on catalyst structures, mechanism, scope and applications of catalytic nitro-Michael additions, conjugate additions to activated alkenes and alkynes, as well as on Mannich type photochemistry and domino/multicat multi-component reactions.
